title: Release version drift: code is 1.0.3 but Unreleased CHANGELOG has user-facing fixes
evidence: ocman.py:191 / pyproject.toml:7 = 1.0.3; CHANGELOG has an [Unreleased] section incl. the TUI-compaction-was-broken fix
impact: Users on PyPI 1.0.3 have broken TUI compaction; the fixes are unreleased
recommended_action: Bump to 1.0.4 (ocman.py + pyproject + ocman_tui fallback) and rename CHANGELOG [Unreleased] -> [1.0.4] with date

title: Confirm target version is 1.0.4 (patch/semver)
evidence: All delta changes are fixes + one additive config key + internal perf; no breaking changes
recommended_action: Assume 1.0.4; confirm with user in final report

title: Committed-secrets scan: no real secrets (built-in flagged 1582 entropy/PII false positives; gitleaks clean)
evidence: Built-in scan_secrets.py: 1582 findings all high-entropy IDs/hashes/timestamps/test-data (credit-card-candidate = epoch-ms/date numbers; generic-secret-env = source literal). gitleaks across 156 commits: 'no leaks found'; detect-secrets available too.
recommended_action: No remediation needed. Optional hardening: add gitleaks to CI (low risk). API keys already never printed + HTTPS-guarded (prior run).
next_step: No fix needed; consider CI gitleaks (S6)

title: _safe_call_from_thread catches RuntimeError broadly
evidence: app.py:1709-1714 catches any RuntimeError from call_from_thread; textual only raises RuntimeError('App is not running') there, but a broad catch could in principle mask an unrelated RuntimeError
impact: Negligible; theoretical masking of an unrelated RuntimeError
recommended_action: Leave as-is: narrowing to message-string match is fragile (Complexity axis) and textual's contract is that call_from_thread raises RuntimeError only for a stopped app
